cms_posts.title: Geo-Blocking & Country Access Control
cms_posts.excerpt: Restrict site access by visitor country, redirect blocked visitors to a lock page, and allow trusted users through with a link-based bypass.
cms_posts.content_html:
Geo-Blocking & Country Access Control

Geo-blocking lets you restrict access to the public site by visitor country. Visitors whose country is not in your allowed list are redirected to a lock page; everyone else is served normally. A link-based bypass lets trusted people (including you) reach the site from a blocked country.

How it works

  • Allowed countries are the countries you enable under Settings → Tax & Countries. Anyone from a country not on that list is blocked.
  • Blocked visitors are redirected to the lock page (default /locked) — build it as an ordinary CMS page.
  • Bypass: a visitor who opens any URL carrying your bypass query (for example ?allowme=yes) is issued a signed cookie and gains full access while that cookie is valid.
  • Enforcement runs in the site's web layer, so it applies to real page views — not just API calls.

Turn it on

  1. Enable the countries you allow under Settings → Tax & Countries.
  2. Create a CMS page for the lock screen (default slug /locked).
  3. Open CMS → Routing Rules → Blocked countries, tick Block access from all other countries, set your bypass query (e.g. allowme=yes), and save.
  4. Make sure the server has a country database installed (see below); until it does, the site stays open (fail-safe) — nothing is ever blocked by mistake.

Country database

Geo-blocking resolves a visitor's country from a MaxMind-format database (GeoLite2-Country.mmdb). Get it free from MaxMind GeoLite2 (account + license key, or the geoipupdate tool for auto-refresh) or from DB-IP IP-to-Country Lite (no account, same format). Place it on the host and it is picked up automatically — no rebuild needed. If it is missing, the site simply serves everyone.

Safety

  • Fail-open by default: if the feature is off, or the database is missing, or a visitor's country can't be determined, they are let through — the site never locks itself out by accident.
  • The admin area stays reachable, and your bypass link always works, so you can't lock yourself out.
  • Country lookup uses the visitor's IP only for the request; no IP addresses are stored.
cms_posts.meta_description: Restrict site access by visitor country, redirect blocked visitors to a lock page, and let trusted users in with a link-based bypass.
cms_posts.meta_keywords: geo-blocking, country access control, geoip, access restriction
